rationale: Both abstracts are strong and essentially report the same study, comparator groups, diagnostic maneuver, and performance metrics. The knowledge gap is specific to distinguishing AT during SVT when pacing responses complicate diagnosis, though it could be more explicitly falsifiable. Results are rigorous for an abstract because they include denominators, comparator groups, sensitivity, specificity, PPV, and NPV, but neither provides confidence intervals or p values. Conclusions are mostly calibrated to a prospective diagnostic study, though “effective” is a little strong given the small AT sample. Y is better overall because it is tighter, more polished, and closer to JACC structured abstract style with uppercase section labels and clearer compression of methods and results.
loser_weakness: X most lacked editorial polish: it is slightly wordier, less aligned with JACC house style, and less concise in the methods and results presentation.
